title: The Silent Echo of the Study
openingIncident: At 8:45 pm, Eleanor Whitcombe, renowned art curator, was found dead in the cramped study littered with a shoe, a remote control, a water bottle and a backpack. Three people had been present during the final minutes, and each gave a different account. Nothing outside the room is needed to identify the killer.

suspects.name: Gareth Hargreaves
suspects.statement: At 8:35 pm, Miriam Calder helped me move the remote control aside. We left the room together immediately afterwards. Trevor Langford and Eleanor Whitcombe were still inside.
suspects.alibiClaim: Miriam Calder and I were together outside the room from 8:35 pm until the discovery at 8:45 pm.

suspects.name: Miriam Calder
suspects.statement: At 8:35 pm, I helped Gareth Hargreaves move the remote control aside. We then left together and remained together until the body was found at 8:45 pm.
suspects.alibiClaim: Gareth Hargreaves and I were together outside the room from 8:35 pm until the discovery at 8:45 pm.

suspects.name: Trevor Langford
suspects.statement: I left at 8:30 pm. I never touched the water bottle and did not go near the shoe.
suspects.alibiClaim: I had already left the room by 8:30 pm and did not return.

clues.title: Granules in Water Bottle
clues.detail: Undissolved blue granules cling below the liquid line inside the water bottle. The same blue residue is visible at Eleanor Whitcombe's lips and in the remaining drink.

clues.title: Position of Remote Control
clues.detail: The fresh contact mark left by the remote control is crossed by a narrow blue droplet from the spilled poisoned drink whose edge remains unbroken. Gareth Hargreaves and Miriam Calder both state that they moved the remote control aside at 8:35 pm before leaving together.

clues.title: Fibres on Water Bottle
clues.detail: Teal-and-cream tweed fibres are caught around the handle and rim of the water bottle. Trevor Langford's teal tweed jacket cuff has a newly frayed cuff edge, exposing the same unusual teal-and-cream check.

clues.title: Fragment at Shoe
clues.detail: A curved black lacquer chip edged in gold is lodged in a narrow edge of the shoe on top of a narrow blue droplet from the spilled poisoned drink. The black-and-gold fountain-pen cap worn by Trevor Langford has a fresh curved gap in the lacquer; the exposed edges visibly fit the fragment.

clues.title: Mark on Backpack
clues.detail: A dark mark crosses the backpack. The same intact factory finish continues over the marked area.

solution.motive: Trevor Langford killed Eleanor Whitcombe because the discovery that the victim had embezzled a six‑figure sum from their shared investment portfolio.
solution.method: A fatal crushed dose was dissolved in the water bottle before Eleanor Whitcombe drank.
solution.timeOfDeath: After 8:35 pm and before discovery at 8:45 pm
solution.opportunity: Trevor Langford claimed to have left at 8:30 pm. The timeline evidence places the attack after Gareth Hargreaves and Miriam Calder left together at 8:35 pm; the teal-and-cream tweed fibres on the water bottle and the black and gold fragment recovered from the shoe place Trevor Langford inside afterwards. Trevor Langford therefore had the only evidenced opportunity to be alone with Eleanor Whitcombe and reach the water bottle during the murder window.
